query_summary: Combined retrieval for porosity with process parameters and user question.

results.title: Gas Porosity Mitigation in WAAM Aluminum Builds
results.text: Stable argon shielding and reduced heat input lowered pore formation during WAAM deposition, especially in aluminum walls.

extracted_evidence.paper: Gas Porosity Mitigation in WAAM Aluminum Builds
extracted_evidence.strategy: Stable argon shielding and reduced heat input lowered pore formation during WAAM deposition, especially in aluminum walls.
extracted_evidence.mechanism: Excess heat input destabilizes the melt pool and promotes pore formation.

parameter_effects.relationship_text: Reduced heat input lowered pore formation during WAAM deposition, especially in aluminum walls.
parameter_effects.recommended_reasoning_hint: Check whether heat input is too high relative to the defect mechanism.

extracted_evidence.experimental_outcome: Stable argon shielding and reduced heat input lowered pore formation during WAAM deposition, especially in aluminum walls.
extracted_evidence.recommendation: Stable argon shielding and reduced heat input lowered pore formation during WAAM deposition, especially in aluminum walls.

reasoning_hints: Check whether the current-voltage-travel speed combination implies excessive heat input.
Evaluate shielding gas flow and composition as a direct contributor to pore formation.
